headline: TASK-076 is the live v6.7 frontier after 6.6.0.

current_state.operating_goal: Advance v6.7 Verifiable Governance: make missing archmap impact dispositions, self-attested review provenance, and low-independence evidence machine-detectable so release sign-off can fail on structural self-attestation instead of trusting maker claims.
current_state.next_value_slice: Close TASK-076 v6.7.0 release: refresh release docs/manifests to 6.7.0, run release-state-audit with integrated Archmap impact, review provenance, and evidence independence gates, run full tests/smoke/help/organization verification, and prepare tag/release notes. Stop condition: release closure only; no new governance audit families. QIF boundary: v6.7 release readiness is structural/runtime governance evidence, not proof of semantic product quality.

primary_frontier_task.title: v6.7 release closure: ship Verifiable Governance

visible_state.speech_bubble: I need council review before I should execute.
visible_state.next_action: Submit the gate packet to council review before actor execution.

why_now.summary: TASK-076 is the best current frontier because it matches the live post-release goal surface and no stronger truth conflict is active.
reasons: The active release baseline is 6.6.0 and the runtime is now targeting v6.7.
The current operating goal and next value slice both reinforce TASK-076 as the next bounded operating move.
